creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jan Beich from comment #5)
...
> After a lot of testing to debug my environment. As part of it I wanted to
> see what code ext/POSIX/Makefile.PL generates. Trying to use |temp0 = 128|
> or |temp0 = sysconf(3)| in the code helped. This suggested clang miscompiles
> it only when __weak_reference happens.
> 
> or
> 
> (lldb) f 1
> frame #1: 0x000000080260cf88 POSIX.so`boot_POSIX(cv=0x000000080215f810) +
> 7448 at const-xs.inc:1140
>    1137 #ifdef CLK_TCK
>    1138         {
>    1139             IV temp0;
> -> 1140             temp0 = CLK_TCK;
>    1141             constant_add_symbol(aTHX_  symbol_table, "CLK_TCK",
>    1142                                     7, newSViv(temp0));
>    1143         }

Okay, let me put it in a different way: what is the exact problem occurring on this line? Does it call into garbage? Is this maybe due to a bug in the patch you took from bug 78787 (specifically because bde points out lots of problems with that patch)? Are we sure that we want to use weak references from header files, while other system headers might disagree?

We really need some sort of minimized, reproducible test case, otherwise I would just suggest to close this PR as WORKSFORME.  It does work with a default, non-patched FreeBSD system.
